close_reason: Absorbed by bedrock-22xb (consolidate MetadataMerge into RoutingData)
description:
Extend MetadataMerge to update routing_data.log_map alongside metadata.log_services.

## Current Behavior

When `{:set, layout_log(log_id), otp_ref}` arrives:
- Stores in `metadata.log_services[log_id]` = service_ref

## New Behavior

When `{:set, layout_log(log_id), otp_ref}` arrives:
1. Store in `metadata.log_services` (current behavior)
2. Also call `RoutingData.insert_log(routing_data, log_id, next_index)`

## Challenge: Index Assignment

log_map uses indices: `%{0 => "log-1", 1 => "log-2", ...}`

Options:
1. Parse index from log_id if it encodes one
2. Assign indices in order of arrival (simple)
3. Store index in the metadata value itself

**Recommended**: Check if SystemKeys.layout_log encodes an index, or the value contains index info.

## Changes to metadata_merge.ex

```elixir
defp apply_mutation({:set, key, value}, metadata, routing_data) do
  case SystemKeys.parse_key(key) do
    {:layout_log, log_id} ->
      service_ref = OtpRef.to_tuple(value)
      # NEW: Also update log_map
      index = extract_log_index(log_id, value)  # or next available
      RoutingData.insert_log(routing_data, log_id, index)
      put_in(metadata, [Access.key(:log_services, %{}), log_id], service_ref)
    # ...
  end
end
```

## Testing

- Verify log_map is updated when layout_log mutation processed
- Verify indices are assigned correctly
- Verify remove on clear
title: MetadataMerge: Update log_map when processing layout_log
